**Verdict: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS** — does not block pack or goal closeout.

The descriptor-only HRX bridge pack (4 AIReviewQueue tail + 6 AdminPolicy foundation rows) is sound and internally consistent across the binding, validators, tests, regenerated contract JSON, plan, and script wiring. Every runtime/write/real-data/final-judgment boundary is enforced at multiple layers, Claude stays a read-only reviewer, and Hermes is evidence-only (no product runtime). The pack honestly reports `production_ready=false`/`in_progress` pending this review, and its scoped `descriptor_verified` claim is fully supported.

The single P3 is a pre-existing, non-actionable note about the contract JSON's nested content being spot-checked rather than deep-diffed against the generator — no actual divergence exists in this pack. All descriptor-only boundaries hold: every runtime/record-write/review-action/rule-execution/policy-finalization/final-judgment flag is false, and no real employee/candidate/payroll data or credentials/secrets are present — enforced in createHrxDomainRows and the validateHrxCp928AiReviewAdminPolicyBridgeCoverage/Descriptor validators (validators.js:5433/5472) and contract validator (validators.js:5588). README and package.json script wiring (full validation ladder + hrx tests in npm test) are present. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer (claude_is_final_approval=false, claude_packet.read_only=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false); Hermes is used only as a deterministic review/evidence packet (emits_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. The final validation ladder, adjudication, construction inspection, and human enterprise-trust approval remain expected post-review steps and are correctly left open (the pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim). No P0/P1/P2 findings; one non-blocking P3 informational note.","findings":[{"id":"CP00-928-P3-01","severity":"P3","title":"Contract JSON nested projection is spot-checked, not deep-diffed against the deterministic generator","file":"packages/hrx/src/validators.js","line":5604,"details":"validateHrxPeopleContract regenerates the CP928 descriptor from code (validateHrxCp928AiReviewAdminPolicyBridgeDescriptor(undefined, contract)) and validates that generated object, while only asserting a few top-level fields of the committed contracts/hrx-people-contract.json (schema_version, program_contract, latest_pack.pack_id, latest_projection.descriptor, validation.valid). The deeply nested stored rows under latest_projection and projections.cp928 in the ~57k-line JSON are not reconciled byte-for-byte against the generator, and the contract test in model.test.js:1706 likewise only checks top-level fields. A stale or hand-edited nested region of the committed artifact would therefore not be caught by rp30:hrx:validate alone. Spot-checks during this review confirm the JSON is correctly regenerated for CP00-928 (latest_pack CP00-928 at line 64, latest_projection HrxCp928AiReviewAdminPolicyBridgeDescriptor at line 595, case set 2 sections/10 rows at line 648, validation.valid=true at line 57035), so there is no actual divergence in this pack.","recommendation":"Optional and non-blocking: consider asserting deep equality between createHrxPeopleContractProjection() output and the committed contracts/hrx-people-contract.json in a test or in validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs to detect drift in the stored artifact.
